metadata.author: Fire Protection Engineering Consultant
metadata.purpose: Conventional Fire Alarm System - Complete Reference

system_overview.name: Conventional Fire Alarm System
system_overview.description: Traditional zone-based fire alarm system where detection devices are grouped into zones. When any device in a zone activates, the entire zone goes into alarm.
system_overview.detection_method: Binary - either normal or alarm state
system_overview.device_identification: Zone level only - no specific device address

active_current_devices.description: Devices that draw standby current and require supervision current budgeting

dry_contact_devices.description: Passive devices with zero standby current - normally-open dry contacts that only close during alarm
dry_contact_devices.quantity_per_zone_note: No fixed numeric limit per NFPA 72. Quantity limited only by total circuit resistance staying within panel rating (typically 50-100 ohms loop resistance). A dry contact adds zero standby current burden.

initiating_circuits_idc.description: IDC - Initiating Device Circuits (smoke detectors, heat detectors, pull stations, waterflow switches)
initiating_circuits_idc.critical_rule: NO_POLARITY_REVERSAL_ON_IDC - alarm detected by current increase only

notification_circuits_nac.description: NAC - Notification Appliance Circuits (horns, strobes, bells, horn-strobes)
notification_circuits_nac.critical_rule: Polarity reversal is ONLY for NAC circuits to prevent device activation during supervision

strict_routing_constraints.description: Routing engine must enforce these constraints to maintain circuit supervision integrity
strict_routing_constraints.violation_consequence: LOSS_OF_SUPERVISION_SILENT_FAILURE - panel cannot detect wire breaks on unsupervised branches

limitations.no_device_address: Cannot identify which specific device activated
limitations.zone_size: Entire zone in alarm - cannot isolate
limitations.no_advanced_features: No drift compensation, no maintenance alerts
limitations.supervision_critical: Class B circuits require strict daisy-chain wiring. Any deviation (T-tap, home-run) creates unsupervised branches where wire breaks go undetected.

elevator.limitation: Recall to floor, not specific elevator
